resultRaw.sql:
WITH per_student_type AS (
  SELECT
    s.student_id,
    s.scholarship_type::text AS scholarship_type,
    MAX(st.gpa) AS gpa
  FROM public.scholarships s
  JOIN public.students st ON s.student_id = st.id
  WHERE st.gpa IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY s.student_id, s.scholarship_type
)
SELECT
  scholarship_type AS scholarship_type,
  AVG(gpa) AS avg_gpa,
  COUNT(student_id) AS student_count
FROM per_student_type
GROUP BY scholarship_type
ORDER BY scholarship_type;

issues: All encoded fields (scholarship_type, avg_gpa, student_count) exist in metadata.columns — OK.

The SQL groups by scholarship_type and returns one row per scholarship_type; the bar chart uses scholarship_type on the nominal x-axis and avg_gpa on the quantitative y-axis — matches aggregation/grain.

Color encodes student_count (quantitative) which is valid, but using color for a quantitative measure can be misleading; consider using a separate size/label or sorting bars and keeping a single color.
The y-axis includes scale.zero: true. For GPA (typically in a narrow high range), forcing zero may visually compress differences; consider removing zero baseline to better show variation.
If you prefer bars ordered by avg_gpa (or student_count) rather than alphabetical scholarship_type, add an explicit sort to the visualization.

result.sql:
WITH per_student_type AS (
  SELECT
    s.student_id,
    s.scholarship_type::text AS scholarship_type,
    MAX(st.gpa) AS gpa
  FROM public.scholarships s
  JOIN public.students st ON s.student_id = st.id
  WHERE st.gpa IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY s.student_id, s.scholarship_type
)
SELECT
  scholarship_type AS scholarship_type,
  AVG(gpa) AS avg_gpa,
  COUNT(student_id) AS student_count
FROM per_student_type
GROUP BY scholarship_type
ORDER BY scholarship_type;

issues: All encoded fields (scholarship_type, avg_gpa, student_count) exist in metadata.columns — OK.

The SQL groups by scholarship_type and returns one row per scholarship_type; the bar chart uses scholarship_type on the nominal x-axis and avg_gpa on the quantitative y-axis — matches aggregation/grain.

Color encodes student_count (quantitative) which is valid, but using color for a quantitative measure can be misleading; consider using a separate size/label or sorting bars and keeping a single color.
The y-axis includes scale.zero: true. For GPA (typically in a narrow high range), forcing zero may visually compress differences; consider removing zero baseline to better show variation.
If you prefer bars ordered by avg_gpa (or student_count) rather than alphabetical scholarship_type, add an explicit sort to the visualization.
